topic: A person accidentally sends a text to the wrong recipient
raw_output:
[story 1]

My thumb hovered over the screen, a moment of sheer, unadulterated panic seizing my chest. I’d meant to send that carefully worded critique to Sarah, my colleague, but my finger slipped, landing squarely on Mark’s contact. A beat of silence stretched, thick and suffocating. Then, the notification popped up: "Hey, are you free for lunch tomorrow?" My stomach clenched. I stared at the screen, the words I’d typed—a pointed observation about the project timeline—now hanging in the air, directed at the wrong person. A flush crept up my neck, hot and undeniable. I quickly tried to delete the message, but the system was slow. I felt a tightening in my jaw, a sudden, sharp desire to retreat entirely.

[story 2]

"Oh, for crying out loud," I muttered under my breath, the sound barely audible over the coffee shop chatter. I had intended that witty retort for my friend, Liam, but my phone had betrayed me, sending it straight to my boss, Mr. Henderson. When the reply came back—a curt, one-word acknowledgment—my hands started to shake. I gripped my lukewarm latte so tightly my knuckles turned white. I took a slow, deliberate sip, trying to steady my breathing, but every muscle in my body felt coiled and tense. I pushed my chair back, the scrape against the tile sounding unnecessarily loud. I needed to walk away, needed distance, because the heat rising in my cheeks felt like a physical burn.

[story 3]

I reread the text before I hit send, a frantic loop of self-reproach playing in my mind. It was a clumsy mistake, a genuine slip of the finger, but the consequence was immediate and deeply unpleasant. The recipient, who was clearly not the intended target, had responded with an overly cheerful emoji. A low, frustrated sound escaped my throat. I slammed my phone face-down on the table, the small impact echoing in the quiet corner. I stood up abruptly, my posture rigid, every line of my body screaming a silent protest. I could feel the blood rushing to my face, a distinct warmth spreading across my skin that made me want to physically shrink away from the entire room.
